content.full_text:
May 2, 1991

Susan M. Schectman  
City Attorney  
Office of the City Attorney  
City of Pacifica  
170 Santa Maria Avenue  
Pacifica, CA 94044

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-91-157

Dear Ms. Schectman:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversations regarding Councilmember Bonnie Wells and whether she may approve payment of the city's monthly PG&E utility bills since she is an employee of PG&E. I sent you a copy of the O'Shea Advice Letter, No. I-88-332, which concluded that a councilmember, employed by Southern California Edison, must disqualify himself from voting on payments for routine electric utility bills from Edison, unless it is determined that the decision regarding payment of the bills will have no financial effect on Edison.

Since the advice in the O'Shea letter directly addresses your question, you stated that it would suffice and you do not need a written advice letter.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)-322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Jill R. Stecher  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JRS:plh  
Enclosure
